finding_results.notes: Chamber B now produces an irreversible legal state change on-page (deferment formally withdrawn and service copy delivered) before reconvening, and duplicate stability restatements were removed.

Fallout is enacted in-scene: marshal enforces legal restrictions through passport surrender and biometric release paperwork; Ji-eun receives live booking cancellation plus escrow setoff; Ina then encounters immediate account hold constraints.
Plaza sequence adds a concrete injury beat and a specific tactical containment order (wedge plus medical lane funnel) that visibly causes crowd retreat.
Institutional tension is now rendered via procedural micro-actions (annotation and microphone repositioning) rather than freeze-template phrasing.
Removed the 'landed like' metaphor template and retained pressure escalation through room-response physicality.
Kwon-vs-Ina exchange now turns on hearing-specific evidence handling and chain-of-custody claims rather than recycled abstraction rhetoric.

summary: Revised chapter 17 to resolve all structural/craft findings in the packet: tightened Chamber B with an irreversible legal turn, strengthened riot-scene causality and injury presence, removed recurring formula constructions, and converted late-stage legal/economic consequences from summary notices into enacted on-page events while preserving continuity and voice constraints.
